channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: INE Security Launches AI-Powered Cybersecurity Training Initiative
item.description: INE Security launches a new initiative to help organizations rethink cybersecurity training and workforce development in the face of AI-powered cyber threats.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Java hiring plans slip, survey says
item.description: Slightly more than half of Java shops surveyed (51%) have plans to add more Java developers in the coming year, down from 60% in 2024, according to a report by Perforce, a provider of Java development and devops tools.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intel taps industry veteran as newest CEO in latest comeback attempt
item.description: Struggling chipmaker Intel has hired former board member and semiconductor industry veteran Lip-Bu Tan as the latest in a succession of CEOs to attempt to turn around a once-dominant company that helped define Silicon Valley.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google signals support for app store age verification law with changes
item.description: Google is pushing back against other major technology companies' efforts to put the responsibility of age verification on the app stores, arguing the onus is on both the stores and developers to boost children's safety.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: StudentAid.gov website went down, following mass layoffs at Department of Education
item.description: An hourslong outage Wednesday on StudentAid.gov, the federal website for student loans and financial aid, underscored the risks in rapidly gutting the Department of Education, as President Donald Trump aims to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Taking the Hassle Out of Personal Storage: Verizon and AT&T Upgrades
item.description: Learn about Verizon and AT&T's unlimited cloud storage upgrades for individuals and groups, including AI photo editing tools and automatic backup capabilities." char-set="UTF-8" scheme="normal
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Black Mirror Season 7: Everything We Know So Far - Netflix's Hit Show Returns | Tech News
item.description: Get the latest news on Black Mirror season 7, including the first look trailer, cast announcements, and updates on upcoming episodes.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: DOGE may be using an algorithm to fire federal workers
item.description: In the past month and a half, the Trump Administration has drastically reduced the federal government workforce. The cuts alone have generated concern and anger among workers and those who rely on US government services.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Toyota's self-charging concept EV could help you tackle the daily commute on solar power alone
item.description: Toyota's new micro mobility concept has a solar roof that it says can generate 20 miles of range per day.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Oversight Democrats launch review of crypto reserve 'poised to enrich' Trump
item.description: House Oversight Democrats are encouraging President Trump to abandon his plans for a crypto reserve, calling it a strategy to enrich himself at taxpayer expense.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Intel shares jump after naming chip industry veteran Tan CEO
item.description: Shares of Intel Corp. jumped the most in five years after the company named Lip-Bu Tan as its next chief executive officer, entrusting a former board member and semiconductor veteran with one of the toughest jobs in the chip industry.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: 40% of IT leaders scared to admit mistakes due to workplace culture of fear
item.description: New research claims many IT leaders are scared to admit mistakes, leading to potentially “major IT incident[s].
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: MrBeast’s chocolate empire is making more money than his YouTube channel
item.description: MrBeast is currently the most-subscribed YouTuber in the world—but his biggest moneymaker isn’t content. It’s chocolate.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: An inside look at Las Vegas valley’s first AI-powered hotel
item.description: Otonomous Hotel, under construction at Russell and Decatur, uses machine learning to create a personalized experience for guests.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Tisminezky, NewsNation

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: No, your printer isn't possessed: a Windows 11 23H2 bug could be making it print random characters when connected via USB
item.description: It might seem to be a strange phenomenon at first, but if your printer has been cranking out random characters lately, it's because of Windows 11 23H2.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft faces FTC antitrust probe over AI and licensing practices
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) is reportedly pressing ahead with an antitrust investigation into Microsoft, a move that could reshape competition in AI and productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google unveils Gemma 3 multi-modal AI models
item.description: Google DeepMind has introduced Gemma 3, an update to the company’s family of generative AI models, featuring multi-modality that allows the models to analyze images, answer questions about images, identify objects, and perform other tasks that involve analyzing and understanding visual data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Majority of Americans have used AI models like ChatGPT: Survey
item.description: A majority of Americans have used ChatGPT-like artificial intelligence (AI) models according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Google reportedly looking at smart glasses
item.description: After abandoning its Google Glass smart glasses in 2023, Google seems ready to take another kick at the can with what will reportedly be a $115 million acquisition of Canadian startup AdHawk Microsystems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: GitHub to unbundle Advanced Security
item.description: GitHub announced plans to unbundle its GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) product, breaking it up into two standalone products: GitHub Secret Protection and GitHub Code Security. The unbundling is set to happen on April 1.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Box CEO Aaron Levie finds a middle ground on tech policy during Trump’s second term
item.description: In our current political and media environment the loudest voices are the ones that are farthest from the center. Tech hasn’t been spared, with some Silicon Valley leaders drifting right—not only out of ideology, but also pragmatism.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google debuts AI model for robotics, challenging Meta, OpenAI
item.description: Research unit Google DeepMind will release Gemini Robotics, a new branch of its flagship AI model aimed at developing robots.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Designing a dynamic web application with Astro
item.description: Astro.js is among the most popular meta-frameworks for JavaScript developers, and for good reason. Astro makes it relatively easy to structure web applications for maximum effect with minimal effort.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JavaOne 2025 heralds Java’s 30th birthday
item.description: With Java advancing toward its 30th anniversary, Oracle will hold its annual JavaOne conference next week, centering around the Java language, the evolution of the Java platform, and the role of Java in areas such as generative AI and machine learning.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Altera targets low-latency AI edge applications with new FPGA products
item.description: Altera has introduced the latest family of Agilex FPGAs, along with Quartus Prime Pro software, and FPGA AI Suite to enable the rapid development of highly customized embedded systems for use in robotics, factory automation systems, and medical equipment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fortinet reinforces OT network security platform
item.description: Fortinet has bolstered its OT Security Platform to help customers more effectively protect industrial control systems and other operational technology networks from cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Finally: some truth serum for lying genAI chatbots
item.description: Ever since OpenAI made ChatGPT available to the public in late 2022, the large language model (LLM)-based generative AI (genAI) revolution has advanced at an astonishing pace. Two years and four months ago, we had only ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components
item.description: Sean Plankey has been tapped to lead CISA after serving in key cyber roles during Trump's first term. His nomination comes as CISA grapples with recent cuts. The post White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Creepy humanoid robot moves using 1,000 eerie artificial muscles
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Clone Robotics’ Protoclone mimics humans with 1,000 muscles – too human for comfort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2
item.description: Joe Welch, the deputy to the commanding general of the Army Futures Command, said the characteristics of need helps define the problem set for industry. The post How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
